title: Online Hierarchical Policy Learning using Physics Priors for Robot Navigation in Unknown Environments

methodology.description: A modular, hierarchical approach for robot navigation in unknown environments
key_components: Online sparse navigation graph construction from local observations
NTFields integration for detailed cost-to-go maps
Local Eikonal equation solving for obstacle management
Temporal Difference Metric Learning (TDM) for improved convergence
advantages: Compact and efficient representation for rapid global planning
Effective management of obstacles and complex geometries
Alleviates scalability issues of traditional NTFields

results.performance: Outperforms existing methods in large, complex, unknown indoor environments
results.deployment: Successfully deployed on a quadruped robot navigating through multiple rooms and narrow passages

related_work: Traditional mapping approaches (occupancy or SDF maps)
challenges_addressed: Computational overhead in traditional mapping approaches
Memory-intensive computations in PRMs for large environments
Scaling issues and spectral bias in NTFields
